title: geranium molle subsp. brutium
intro: Geranium molle subsp. brutium, also known as Dinaric geranium, is a subspecies of the Geranium molle plant, a type of flowering perennial plant native to the Dinaric Alps in Europe and the Middle East. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Geranium molle subsp. brutium include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe wounds, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
